texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID1: Memo is received, time/date stamped and acknowledged in Director's office by the Office Aide
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID2: Office Aide sends to the Director who minutes/instructs accordingly to officer responsible for related task
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID3: Office Aide dispatches minuted memo to officers responsible OR Office Manager or Administrative Assistant prepares draft memo and sends draft to Director for review
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID4: Director reviews, amends if need be and returns to Office Manager or Administrative Assistant for production of final draft OR Director finalizes, signs and dispatches electronically while copying office email to constitute file copy, for archiving by Office Manager and Office Aide
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID5: Office Aide/ Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant/ Director dispatches to destination (sometimes electronically) while ensuring acknowledgement.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD01:PID6: In the absence of electronic acknowledgement, Office Manager or Administrative Assistant sends a reminder after 3 working days
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR1: Memo is reviewed for receipt by Office Manager for hard copies OR Office Aide/ Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant/ Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR2: Memo without necessary supporting documents/wrongly transmitted is returned to Client Directorate OR Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR3: Memo with all supporting documents is received and acknowledged in incoming register by Office Aide or electronically by Director
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR4: Office Aide submits memo to Director for action with summary of request Director acts on request/minutes memo to appropriate DCP Staff/destination. Director, when acting on request electronically, copies DCP office email
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR5: Office Aide, Office Manager, Administrative Assistant receives treated memo, registers, scans, and produces copy of files Office Aide, Office Manager, Administrative Assistant generates hard copy for dispatch and archiving or for archiving alone
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR7: Office Aide dispatches duly registered memo to destination
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR8: Office Aide delivers memo to destination and obtains acknowledgement in outgoing register
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD02:RICR9: Director/Office Manager and Office Aide continuously review daily dispatch of documents.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN1: Director receives from PPO Protocol soft copy of draft NV with supporting documents where necessary
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN2: Director receives soft copy of NV from Office Manager
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN3: Director reviews send back to PPO Protocol for production of final hard copy while directing Office Manager to provide reference number
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN4: Director receives final version for initialling
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN5: Director dispatches initialled Note Verbale to Protocol through OA/ OM, Admin
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN6: Office Aide / Office Manager obtain Stamp from Office of Vice-President, Director of Cabinet or External Relation
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN7: Initialled Note Verbale is dispatched to Protocol Division
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD03:RNVINN8: Director checks with PPO Protocol/Office Manager or requests acknowledgement copy from Protocol Division
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE1: Administrative Assistant recalls approved dates for Statutory Meetings by …
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE2: Director reviews and returns for production of final draft and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE3: OM submits final correspondence and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE4: Director sends preliminary version of correspondence intended for President’s approval to Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE5: Upon receipt of Supervising Commissioner’s amendments, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE6: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final versions to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE7: Director signs cover memos, while checking again accompanying final versions
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE8: Office Aide dispatches to Supervisoring Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD04:SMOE9: Director reviews all processes for completion with Office Manager, Office Aide and Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE2: Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner’s reaction to the meeting dates, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE3: Director amends and returns for production of final accepted dates and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE4: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final accepted dates to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE5: OM submits accepted dates and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE6: Director sends final accepted dates for President’s information through Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to the EA of Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE7: Once President’s confirmation of of the dates is received, Director informs DCP colleagues
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE8: Director instructs Office Manager to prepare draft correspondence for Management to inform all Community Institutions of the approved dates
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE9: Final versions of memos accompanied by signed cover memo are dispatched to Management
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE10: Director instructs HOD Conference to approach DCP colleagues and support Directorates for expression of needs
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE11: Director instructs HOD Conference to prepare a draft budget reflecting the collated needs, memorandum of understanding on organization of ECOWAS meetings, the approved standard rates for goods and services, the rules of procedure for the governing bodies and the order of precedence
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE12: Director reviews draft budget and where necessary returns to HOD Conference for adjustment.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE13: The reviewed budget reflecting Director’s amendments is returned
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE14: Director instructs the Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant to send the pre-final version of budgets for Statutory Meetings to Management
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE15: Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner’s amendments, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE16: Director amends and returns for production of final draft budgets and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE17: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final draft budgets to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE18: OM submits final draft budget and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE19: Director sends final draft budget intended for President’s approval to Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE20: Director instructs OM/Administrative Assistant to prepare an electronic and modifiable version of List of Participants for circulation to all departments.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE21: Director reviews the List of Participants and instructs for any adjustment to be made.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE22: Director receives the populated list and finalizes by the deadline indicated.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE23: Director sends preliminary version of List of Participants intended for President’s approval to Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE24: Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner’s amendments to the pre-final List of Participants, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE25: Director amends and returns for production of final List of Participants and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE26: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final List of Participants to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE27: OM submits final List of Participants and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE28: Director sends final List of Participants intended for President’s approval to Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE29: Once final approved List of Participants is received, Director instructs the Office Manager to circulate the List of Participants
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE30: Director instructs the Office Manager to send the List of Participants to Communication Directorate for circulation to Staff
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE31: Director instructs the Reproduction Unit to prepare badges for all participants for AFC and Technical Meetings respecting ECOWAS Commission corporates design/ designs to guide Host Government for Ministerial and Summit level meetings
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE32: Director instructs all HOD to submit the schedule of duty of each DCP Staff member for Pre, during and post Statutory Meeting.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE33: Director instructs PPO Protocol to provide list of recommended hotels and negotiated rates.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE34: Director instructs HOD Conference to provide updated and active meeting registration link for insertion in Administrative Note.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE35: Director instructs the Office Manager to prepare and send the draft memo to other support directorates (ITS, DAGS, Communication)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE36: The Administrative Assistant and OM prepares the draft Administrative Note for delegates inserting the list of recommended hotels, ECODOCS Login details and updated meeting registration link and currency exchange rates and expected weather.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE37: The draft Admin Note is submitted to Director for review
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE38: Director instructs Administrative Assistant/OM to electronically dispatch reviewed and approved Administrative Note to delegates.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE39: Once approved meeting dates are received, OM prepares invitation letters and submits to Director - still within PeC’s Purview
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE40: Administrative Assistant referencing archived meetings on ECODOCS and translated draft agendas of upcoming statutory meetings, prepares meeting titles reflecting the correct edition in all 3 working languages.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE41: Document reflecting approved meeting titles and dates is circulated to all relevant stakeholders (National office in host member state, Protocol Division, Conference Division, all staff of DCP, Communication Directorate)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE42: Director convenes initial preparation meeting with HOD and reviews preparations of all deliverables, Including any required liaison with support directorate and deliverables from Director's office itself
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE43: Director convenes meeting with all DCP Staff to review overall preparedness and readiness of Statutory Meetings
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE44: Director request (proposes) and receive dates for meeting with Local Organizing Committee (LOC) and sends signed MOU on the Organisation of ECOWAS Meetings to Host Government
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE45: Director instructs Office Manager to prepare memos to invite Staff of the support directorates to participate in the meeting of the LOC, while forwarding to support directorates signed Memorandum of Understanding (Base document for collaboration for Host Government)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE46: Directorate Conference and Protocol leads the ECOWAS Commission delegation to participate in the LOC meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE47: For Statutory Meeting outside HQ, Director approaches management for approval for DCP advance team to proceed for identification and inspection of venues in the member state hosting the meeting or Chairing the Authority - still within PeC’s Purview
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE48: Director reviews facility inspections report and makes recommendation to Management and DAGS for designation and engagement of service providers
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE49: Director invites Management to conduct final check of facilities and arrangements- still within PeC’s Purview
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE50: While meeting/event is ongoing Director monitors smooth functioning of all meeting pillars (payments, bills, documentation, accreditation, signature of final reports, etc)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE51: Director supervises the dismantling of meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD05:PMOE52: Director receives analysis of feedback and assessment forms, and reviews DCP procedures accordingly.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR2: Director receives instructions from Management to prepare contributions
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR3: Director requests Director's Office Support Staff to provide previous versions where it exists/prepare preliminary draft
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR4: Director requests elements from divisions where necessary in electronic version
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR5: Director directs Support Staff to merge all elements and prepare second preliminary draft
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR6: Director reviews second preliminary draft and dispatches to Support Staff/Office Manager/Documentation Division for formatting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR8: Director forwards DCP contribution to appropriate quarters (electronic version or hard copy)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD06:PRCR9: All DCP contributions to reports are available and accurate
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD07:PCNTR2: Director receives instruction from Management/Technical directorate
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD07:PCNTR3: Director requests draft concept note/terms of reference from relevant HOD / Team Leads in implementation of approved budget/activity plan
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD07:PCNTR4: Director reviews draft received and instructed relevant HOD/Team Leads to prepare final version
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD07:PCNTR5: Director dispatches completed document to appropriate quarters
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD07:PCNTR6: Director ensures all concept note and TOR relating to activities in the approved work plan is available
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM1: Director instructs Office Manager or Administrative Assistant to prepare relevant background information, notice of meeting, draft agenda and work program
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM2: Director reviews preliminary background information and supporting document, amending where necessary or issuing instruction to DCP Staff for finalisation.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM3: Director directs Office Manager to dispatch Notice of Meeting, Meeting Link, Agenda, Work Program and any supporting documents
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM4: Director instructs Office Manager or Administrative Assistant to prepare action points for Director's/ HOD/Team Lead's own use
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM5: Office Manager or Administrative Assistant activates meeting recording and transcription on the virtual platform
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM7: Office Manager or Administrative Assistant listens to the recordings of the meeting and generates action points, and agenda of the next meeting, then sends to the Director for review
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM8: Director reviews the report of meeting, and action points to the Office Manager or Administrative Assistant
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD08:PIM9: Director reviews number of action point treated
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM1: Director receives approved draft agenda from Management
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM2: Director sends approved agenda to ECODOCS Team for review of details of Status of Documents spreadsheet.  Submit Draft Agenda for Management’s review and clearance Indicate document availability on ECODOCS to Management and facilitate session with Secretary General and President’s Office to jointly review Status of Documents and Translation
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM3: Director instructs Support Staff to continuously adjust Status of Documents in line with President's instructions
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM4: Director instructs ECODOCS Team to format and upload documents in all three languages as against President's approval of agenda items Director continues to release documents as upload is completed by Support Staff
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM5: Director remains available to instruct ECODOCS Team for replacement/removal of any uploaded document
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM6: ECODOCS Team checks that system has automatically archived all documents and reviews meeting identifying details in the backend
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:OFD:OD09:DUPMEPM7: Director checks the availability of all documents on the relevant ECODOCS page of the meeting.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE1: Conference division through DCP requests for the activities for the quarter from office of the President
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE2: HOD assigns a Conference Assistant to extract detailed information from the approved work plan
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE3: Assigned Conference Assistant draws up the draft quarterly calendar of events.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE4: Conference Assistant submits draft quarterly calendar to HOD for vetting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE5: HOD sends draft calendar to the director for approval and circulates to other directorates
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE6: HOD allocates each conference assistance to a specific directorate(s)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE7: Each Conference Assistant to liaise with his allocated directorate to confirm the meetings in the quarterly calendar and update with logistics (venue/ venue/ amount of days/number of participants)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF01:CCOE8: Assigned Conference Assistant undertakes checks at end of every month and update calendar when necessary. collation continues
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF02:BP1: HOD receives instruction from Director to prepare budgets for all statutory /sectoral ministerial meetings
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF02:BP2: HOD instructs Conference Assistant to contact other DCP divisions/ support directorates involved, also client directorate to obtain all details necessary for budget preparation.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF02:BP3: Conference Assistant prepares the draft budget and submits to HOD for vetting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF02:BP4: HOD sends the prepared budget to Director for onward transmission to the technical directorate.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC1: HOD receives approved memo/ meeting request form from Director
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC2: HOD minutes of the meeting request to Conference Assistant in charge of necessary logistics preparation
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC3: Conference Assistant completes the logistics request form as against the approved budget and submits it to Director through the HOD
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC4: Director approves and sends to admin directorate
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC5: Logistics requested received from General Administrationistration and deployed by Conference Assistant assigned to the meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC6: Conference Assistant requests and receives menu from identified caterer(s) and submits to HOD
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF03:MLC7: HOD with participation of Conference Assistant calls for meeting with caterer to discuss identified service points, timings, food distribution, presentation, packaging, number of waITS Staff, waste disposal etc.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF04:EMME1: Conference Assistant holds duty station in the hall
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF04:EMME2: Conference Assistant supervises Support Staff and monitors the smooth running of the meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF04:EMME3: Conference Assistant supervises food and ensures timely delivery of food to meeting venue and informs focal persons whenever food is ready
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM1: Conference Staff requests the bills from the service providers
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM2: Conference Staff vet bills in line with the approved budget and submit to Director/coordinator for approval for onward transmission to Gen. Admin for payment preparation.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM3: Conference Staff fills feedback assessment form and submits it to the HOD conference for onward transmission to Director
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM4: Conference Staff fills out the service certification form and submits it to HOD for review and signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM5: Conference Staff ushers the service provider to the accountant for the payment
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM6: Conference Assistant takes a random number of feedback forms as a basis for analysing the service
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM7: Conference Assistant analyses the filled feedback forms
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM8: Conference Assistant presents and hands over to HOD for onward transmission to Director
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM9: Conference Assistant, Reproduction Assistant initiate clearance of all conference materials by instructing ad hoc staff on the collection of all leftover documents and meeting materials
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM10: Conference Assistant, Reproduction Assistant with the help of Support Staff clear all used conference materials from all meeting spaces
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:CONF:CONF05:MCPM11: Reproduction/Conference Assistants ensure proper shredding of all leftover documents

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT1: Officer receives approved meeting/transportation request from supervisor.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT2: Officer makes hotel reservation for participants or delegates
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT3: Officer confirms that Admin note was sent to the delegates from the client directorate, capturing all relevant information that the delegate needs to be aware of before their arrival at the port of entry or pick up.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT4: For Road Transportation, Officer completes the transport request forms and submits to the General Administrationistration Directorate for official vehicle assignment.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT5: For Ferry or rail transportation, Officer confirms travel schedules, requirements (conditions for travel) and cost of travel before communicating to client and processing the purchase of Ferry/Train Tickets
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT6: Officer arranges transportation of participants to the station and to the intended destination
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT7: Officer follows up with Travel Unit to obtain their travel itineraries.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT8: Reception and Departure Ceremonies RECEPTION: Confirm availability of placards and Flap Jackets for Protcol Officer to wear to the airport for proper identification of Protocol Officer. Be at the Airport latest, 30mins before scheduled arrival of guests. Stand at the designated area holding the banner/placard while vehicle is positioned ready to receive the guests. Guests are received and ushered to the vehicle while ensuring at least 1 Officer is always at the designated point to receive the guests If the guest does not show up, the client directorate is informed accordingly to figure out what might have happened while the delegates received are appealed to, to wait for 10 mins to confirm location of the delegates yet to be received. Protocol approaches MFA Staff for support to receive high level delegates.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT9: Official Events and Ceremonies Protoco Makes sure delegates are picked up to arrive meeting venue from deisgnated accomodation latest 30mins before commencement of meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT10: Diplomatic Missions and Visits. Protocol confirms the guest list and confirms that venue and conveniences are cleaned and set up with red carpet laid for the reception of VIP. (
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT11: Officer obtains driver assignment from Admin, initiates and monitors his prepared schedules.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT12: Protocol would confirm that General Administrationistration has checked valid driver’s licence, any visible signs of wear and tear, dressing of driver, his command of English or whichever language of the country the meeting is holding. This is where the vehicle inspection would be detailed.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT01:GT13: Delegation transported and assignment completion reported to supervisor
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP1: Officer receives approved memo for visa request from supervisor.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP2: Officer contacts client and provides requisite checklist.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP3: Officer prepares draft Note Verbale and submits it to the supervisor for review and onward transmission to DDCP for final approval.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP4: Note Verbale is reviewed by DDCP and approval given for issuance of number.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP5: NV is registered in the log, the NV is stamped and submitted
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP6: When is the NV registered in the NV log book?
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP7: Consular Officer Submits Note Verbale to the appropriate channel (i.e. Embassy, Consulate or High Commission) with fully filled checklist and supporting documentation
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP8: An acknowledgement copy is collected. and used to followup on the request after 2weeks of submission
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP9: Officer schedules visa interview appointment with the embassy.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP10: Communicate appointment details to the client and ensure client attends the appointment as scheduled.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP11: Officer or Client picks up passport with issued visa
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP12: All movements to the embassies are conveying Protocol Officers and applicants are done with ECOWAS Official Vehicles .
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT02:VP13: Report completion of the assignment to the supervisor
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM2: Officer receives memo from DCP notifying of the event
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM4: Assigned Officer contacts External Relations for list of expected delegations containing their full names and official designations and their ETA at the Commission.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM5: Send email to the relevant divisions to confirm their awareness of upcoming event: Security Division for access, driveway and parking arrangements, Maintenance Division for venue cleaning services and any necessaryrepairs. Conference Division for water and light refreshments as required.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM6: PPO assigns roles to Protocol Staff to support the event accordingly i; Manning the 2 gates for entry and exit. Ii. Manning the red carpet to accompany the Head Of Mission to the Holding room
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM7: Final inspection: The venue setup is checked one more time By whom?
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM9: Each delegation is escorted to the briefing room as they arrive By whomby designated Staff.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM10: Each delegation is presented to the President of the Commission by the PPO Protocol or Consular Officer and accredited. By whom
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM11: After accreditation, each Head of Mission is escorted for departure. By PPO Protocol or designated Staff
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM12: PPO holds briefing exercise with relevant units in the Directorate of Administration and General services and Conference Division on their readiness for upcoming event.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM13: Protocol confirms Security arrangement and venue preparationBy General Administrationistrationistration
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT03:AHM14: DCP notified of completion of the assignment
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC2: PPO sends the memo proposing budget, dates, theme, venue (which normally should always be at the Commission)and draft list of invitees to the President through DCP.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC3: PPO receives approved memo confirming budget, dates, venue and list of invitees from the office of the President through DCP.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC4: Protocol in collaboration with reprographic unit prepares the invitations and The design
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC5: Invitations are addressed and dispatched according to the approved guest list.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC6: Protocol Division, in liaison with External Relations, prepares and reviews talking points for the President, which are submitted for DCP’s review and onward transmission to Management
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC7: PPO Prot hosts a preparation meeting and assigns responsibilities.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC8: Venue is prepared by Gen Admin and Conference based on Protocol’s recommendations
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC9: Finalisation of logistic arrangement, final sweep of venue of event a day before, and on the day of the event. /Who does this and what is the sweep for? Security? Event paraphernalia? Also there has to be liaison here with some other Divisions. They should be mentioned.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC10: President of the Commission arrives before the event to receive his guests at the entrance of the venue. The President would be accompanied by Director External Relations and PPO Protocol for proper identification of the guests.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC11: The President is guided from guest to guest for the implementation of the talking points.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC13: President makes the only speech and bring the event to a conclusion
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT04:DC15: DCP is notified of completion of assignment

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS3: Protocol Officer makes arrangement for accommodation for 60 days, in hotel with apartments with which Commission has agreement as officially indicated by Directorate General Administrationistrationistration.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS4: Protocol Officer makes arrangement for transportation by carrying out ground transportation SOPand meet new staff at the airport.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS7: Protocol Officer guides new staff on relevant local registrations: NIN, mobile networksby scheduling visit to relevant to relevant authorities
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS8: Designated Protocol Coordinates the introduction of staff at HR for appropriate documentation and
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS9: Protocol Officer facilitates transportation of New Staff to Duty post after documentation.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT05:RLNS10: Protocol Officer vets bill and prepares the payment schedule after receiving the bill from the Hotel through PPO Prot. .

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM2: Protocol Officer participates in a coordination meeting with the Political Affairs Directorate.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM3: Protocol Officer plans reception and accommodation of the Head and members of the observation mission.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM4: Protocol Officer collects necessary documents for the processing of payment to all observers.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM5: Protocol Officer carries out environmental surveillance on the Head of Mission's observation centersto confirm accessibility, capacity and security of the briefing room and observation centres
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM6: Protocol Officer supervises the departure of each team deployed to the regions and remains in touch with them through their team leadsrotocol will remain in touch. for proactive intervention.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM7: Protocol Officer meet designated transport service provider and agree on inspection. WVehicle AC, Cleanliness, Spare tyres and emergency kit, driver’s temperament and dressing, drivers ease of communication are checked as well as relevant driver and vehicle documents are checked
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM8: Protocol Officer liaises with Political Affairs for the deployment of observers using the list of inspected vehicles for logistics.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM10: Protocol Officer liaises with Political Affairs to coordinate briefing of observers.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM11: Protocol Officer liaises with Political Affairs to coordinate debriefing of observers
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT06:EOM12: Protocol Officer liaises with PAPS and relevant authorities for the deployment of security contingencies.  At the end of the mission, the vehicle stickers are withdrawn. Buses are used to convey delegates to the airport according to flight schedules tre? What happens at departure?

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT07:BF2: Protocol Officer interprets memo for composition of the delegation scheduled to cross the borders

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT10:INVP7: Refunds (where applicable) are made to the travel agency for processing.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT10:INVP8: Travel Unit reconciles with the travel agencies monthly.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA1: Receive approval memo for the visa-on-arrival (VOA).
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA2: Check if the date aligns with the 10 working days period prior to the applicant's arrival.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA3: If time constraints arise, advise supervisor accordingly. If sufficient time is available, proceed to contact the applicant for the necessary requirements.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA4: Prepare a formal letter addressed to the Comptroller-General of Immigration.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA7: Submit the documentation at the Directorate of visa and residence at the Nigeria Immigration Service.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA8: Follow-up on the request via telephone call/ physical visit to Directorate of visa and residence at the Nigeria Immigration Service 5 days after submission
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA9: Receive the final approval and provide the necessary documents to the applicant.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT11:OVA11: Report to supervisor upon completion of the assigned tasks.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW1: Officer receives request memo and proceeds to request the original copies from the client.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW2: Officer prepares Note Verbale addressed to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs seeking approval of form CC (duty waiver forms).
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW3: Upon approval by both MFA and Nigerian Customs, Officer mails a copy of the approved documents to the client.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW4: Officer sends electronic copies of letters of authority to the shipping line, Nigeria Customs at the seaport, and Port Authority to facilitate the release of the consignment to the designated clearing agent
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW7: Lagos Liaison Office to carry out an initial physical inspection of the shipped items
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW8: Officer to arrange for transportation of the shipped items from the Lagos seaport to the approved destination.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT12:CDW9: Abuja officer to carry out further inspection in collaboration with staff from client Directorate at the ECOWAS Commission or approved final destination of cleared item(s)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT13:ROR1: Approved memo is received for repatriation from HR
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT13:ROR2: Note Verbale to announce passing of staff to deceased Embassy/High Commission with the Nigerian MFA in copy.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT13:ROR3: Liaise with in-house clinic and HR to confirm all medical documentations for repatriation of the remains
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT13:ROR4: Process and submit letter to FAAN for the repatriation of staff remains
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT13:ROR9: Report and completion of assignment is reported to PPO
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC1: Officer receives a request for a residence permit/card, from PPO Protocol. Officer assesses the request and contacts the applicant to request the necessary documents and payment. The required amount may vary based on the nationality of the applicant.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC2: If request needs to go through Ministry of Foreign Affairs, a Note Verbale is processed
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC3: Officer proceeds to prepare a formal letter addressed to the Comptroller General of Immigration. Letter is proofread by PPO Protocol and signed by DDCP. Letter is stamped by External Relations
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC4: Officer submits relevant documents to the Directorate of Visa and Residence at the Nigeria Immigration office.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC6: Residence permit/card is issued (usually within 24 hours for West Africans and 2 weeks for other Nationalities)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:PROT:PROT14:RSC9: Officer delivers residence cards to the applicant.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS1: Receipt of interpretation service request/MRF minuted by DDCP to LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS2: Language Coordinator (LC) shall minute request to the Language Assistant (LA) for initial assessment
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS3: LA shall assess the validity of the interpretation service request & adequacy of client’s supporting documents (indication of budget/sheet, PR number where client is funding, working documents, venue, working languages, and the number of participants, need for SIE etc.)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS4: LA shall submit the assessed request to LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS5: When the request meets requirements, it is validated to by LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT01:AIS6: If for cogent reasons interpretation cannot be provided (unavailability of staff interpreters and non-indication of budget/ sheet and other relevant documents / information), the LC shall inform the client directorate.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM1: Based on the interpretation service request, LC shall determine language combination, team strengths and SIE or virtual meeting needs
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM2: LC shall check availability of staff Interpreters and assign a team accordingly if they are available.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM3: LA shall acknowledge receipt of the request, inform the client directorate about the action taken, on the instruction of LC give the team leader's contact, request for working documents and any other information.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM4: When staff interpreters are not available and there are adequate budgetary provisions, LC shall instruct LA to send out availability requests to Freelancers as designated by LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM5: Proposed weighted team of interpreters based on response to availability checks sent. to LA (who sends this to the LA? I thought it is the other way round).
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM6: LC shall instruct LA to send out confirmations of offers to freelancers who confirmed their availability
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM7: LC sends memo to DDCP for SIE procurement
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM8: LA shall generate the appropriate contracts, based on team size and language combination (including retour) and submit them to LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM9: LC shall check the contracts before submitting them to DDCP
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM10: Upon receipt of the signed contracts from DDCP, LC shall direct LA to email scanned copies to freelance interpreters for signature
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM11: LA shall send working documents (or give the username and password to access ECODOCS as far as statutory meetings are concerned) and useful links to interpreters concerned
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM12: In the case of a virtual meeting, LA shall obtain the meeting link from Conference Section and send it to interpreters
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT02:PVISRM13: LA shall receive and transmit the signed contracts to meeting focal point and/or finance.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM1: On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare the Language Schedule where staff assignments are indicated and email it to the DDCP, DCP HOD, staff Interpreters and translators.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM2: Once notified of a meeting outside their duty station, staff interpreters shall make their TVM requests on ECOLINK.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM3: On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare ticket request and have it vetted by LC. The ticket request shall then be sent to DDCP who shall sign and forward it to Travel Unit for issuance of tickets.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM4: Should ECOWAS rent a mobile SIE from a supplier, it shall be installed no later than the eve of the meeting, shall be functional and placed in a position where a clear view of the high table/projection screen is provided. In addition, it shall be compatible with the virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM5: LC shall appoint team leaders for meetings. The most senior staff interpreter shall be the de facto team leader. In event that there is no staff interpreter on the team, a freelance interpreter designated by LC shall play that role
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM6: The team leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters i.e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are always maintained among colleagues, etc.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM7: Where practical arrangements are not in place, the Team Leader shall draw the attention of Conference Division or whoever plays that role
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM8: LC obtains confirmation and contact details of SIE provider from HOD CONF/Head Procurement
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM9: LC/Team Leader receives/confirms details of designated driver to convey interpreters to venue of meeting.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM10: LC sets time for sound check ahead of meeting
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM11: Upon installation, the SIE provider shall test the equipment and ensure that it is working. In respect of hybrid meetings, upon installing the required interface and cables, ITS shall logging on to the interpretation platform and do a preliminary sound check to ensure that it is working
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM12: LC as well as interpreters ensure component checks (size and position of booths, booth labels, fans, booth lamps, screen, extension boxes, hybrid interpretation platform interface and cable, swivel chairs, writing materials and stationery, adjoining conducive waiting area for interpreters) and verification of the Installation specifications with Conference Division/the technicians
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM13: Interpreters shall present themselves at the venue of their assigned meetings at least 1 hour to the commencement of the meeting. Sound check shall be done under the team leader's supervision to confirm that the SIE is working.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM14: Check that input and output sound are clear
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM15: check that relay is functional and that interpreters master the use of consoles
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM16: Check that there is direct sound to the interpreters from the roving mics
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM17: Check language settings on consoles and receivers
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM18: Check sound from the interpretation platform
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM19: Team leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the client directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM20: Team leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM21: LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT03:DIPM22: LC shall submit feedback forms to DDCP when there are issues requiring her attention.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM1: LA shall send the meeting link received from the Conference Section to interpreters
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM2: A day before the meeting or during the pre-meeting tests, roles shall be assigned. Each booth shall appoint a Captain, preferably the most senior staff interpreter or a freelancer if there is no staff in the booth.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM3: The Booth Captain shall create a WhatsApp group with all his team members including the DCP Co-host. S/he should prepare a roster (order of work) in consultation with her/his team members.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM4: The Booth Captain is the interface between her/his booth and the DCP Co-host and Host. S/he shall report any challenge brought to her/his attention or that s/he may have observed and that may adversely affect the smooth performance of her/his colleagues
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM5: Each booth shall agree on their preferences as far as symbols are concerned (?: 5mn more; ?: end your sentence; handshake symbol: handover) or Messages (5 min before handover: “GET READY” and when it is time: “REPREND” or “GO”)
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM6: Upon handing over, the interpreter that was ON shall listen to the one who has taken over, from his/her second device, to ensure that the transition is going smoothly.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM7: Interpreters' Cohost is designated by the Host (organizer of the meeting) in consultation with DDCP. S/he is the interface between the three Booth Captains and the Host. S/He shall perform sound check by listening to the various booths and confirming that the sound is loud and clear before the meeting starts. When participants log on, another sound check shall be carried out with them to ensure that they are good to go.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM8: Sound check shall be led by DCP Cohost
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM9: LC shall instruct LA to inform colleague interpreters of the start time of sound check
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM10: Check that input and output sound are clear
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM11: DCP Cohost shall try to resolve challenges that occur and report those beyond his/her control to LC
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM12: In the event of serious difficulties that cannot be resolved at their level LC shall inform DDCP for appropriate action to be taken by ITS
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM13: The participants' audio check shall also be done by LC before any audio output is disseminated
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM14: Team leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the client directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM15: Team leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM16: LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT04:DIVM17: LC shall submit feedback forms to DDCP when there are issues requiring her attention.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM1: On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare the Language Schedule and email it to line managers, staff Interpreters and translators and other DCP Divisions.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM2: Once notified of a meeting outside their duty station, staff interpreters shall make their TVM requests on ECOLINK.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM3: On LC's instructions, LA shall prepare ticket requests and sent them back to him/her for proper verification. The ticket request shall be sent to DDCP who shall sign and forward it to Travel Unit for issuance of tickets.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM4: Should ECOWAS rent a mobile SIE from a supplier, it shall be installed no later than the eve of the meeting, shall be functional and placed in a position where a clear view of the high table/projection screen is provided. In addition, it shall be compatible with the virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM5: The SIE shall be compatible with virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms. ITS / the service provider shall ensure that the SIE is fitted with required interface and cables, logged on to the interpretation platform and ready for use by interpreters.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM6: LC shall appoint the team leader once the team is confirmed. The most senior staff interpreter shall be the de facto team leader. In event that there is no staff interpreter on the team, a freelance interpreter designated by LC shall play that role
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM7: The team leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters, i.e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are maintained at all time among colleagues, etc. In addition, at the time of confirming the offer, the LC/team leader shall inform interpreters of any possible requirements to meet in respect of the virtual meeting platform to be used.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM8: Where practical arrangements are not in place, the Team Leader shall draw the attention of Conference Division or whoever plays that role
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM9: Interpreters shall present themselves at the venue of their assigned meetings at least 1 hour to the commencement of the meeting. Sound check shall be done under the team leader's supervision to confirm that all the sound parameters are functional. Sound check shall be done concurrently with that of the interpretation platform. A simulation of participants’ use of audio on the interpretation platform shall also be done.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM10: Check that input and output sound are clear
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM11: check that relay is functional and that interpreters master the use of consoles
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM12: Check that there is direct sound to the interpreters from the roving mics
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM13: Check language settings on consoles and receivers
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM14: Check sound from the interpretation platform
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM15: The participants' audio check shall also be done by LC before any audio output is disseminated
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM16: Team leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the client directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM17: Team leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM18: LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT05:DIHM19: LC shall submit feedback forms to DDCP when there are issues requiring her attention.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT06:PFISP1: LC shall ensure that all payment requirements are met in due time. To that effect, S/he shall instruct LA to give all relevant information on the payment process to freelance interpreters (and SIE providers) ahead of time and ensure that all relevant supporting documents are submitted in line with ECOWAS financial procedures
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT06:PFISP2: For physical meetings, LA shall ensure early submission of copies of signed contracts and other supporting documents by freelance interpreters (and SIE provider's endorsed proforma invoice) to the Protocol Assistant (PA) or Conference Assistant (CA) on the meeting. The latter in turn shall submit them to the Finance Directorate for timely and hitch-free payment
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT06:PFISP3: When freelance language professionals are not paid at the venue of the meeting or if it is a virtual meeting, LC shall instruct LA to prepare a cover memo with relevant documents for DDCP's attention for processing.
texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:INT:INT06:PFISP4: LC shall instruct LA to follow up outstanding payments. LC shall draw DDCP's attention to delays and give relevant information to language service providers concerned

texts.ServiceWorkflowStep:TRANS:TRANS04:CTFRE1: Language Assistant, on instruction of Language Coordinator sends request to destination language Revisor
